status: Post-hoc falsification amendment frozen after inspecting the first rigorous graph outputs and before computing the diagnostics below
reason: The disjoint signed-relation graph was strong, but outcome direction is partially associated with answer presentation order within surface variants. The following diagnostics test whether the result is only an answer-order or variant-matching artifact.
diagnostics: Report selected-edge precision separately for every ordered source-variant to target-variant pair.
Report selected-edge precision and edge counts separately for same versus opposite answer presentation order.
Report the fraction of correct and incorrect graph edges that preserve answer presentation order.
Report outcome precision for a deterministic answer-order-only similarity baseline.

Report outcome precision for a numeric-direction-only oracle similarity; this is an interpretive ceiling showing what can be solved by parsing increase versus decrease after mechanism family is supplied by graph stratification.
Report paired family-level contrasts of Jacobian against direct, raw, word TF-IDF, and character TF-IDF.
Retain all six families and all ordered edge types.
additional_heldout_robustness: Report union and mutual undirected cross-phrasing graph homophily.
Report pairwise directed edge-set Jaccard overlap among the three lens fits.
interpretation_rule: The signed-relation graph is strong only if both counterfactual-crossing edge types and same-surface edge types exceed chance. Equality with direct or raw states must be reported as lack of Jacobian specificity.
